title: Post Toasties pudding – Ah, those days in the boarding house
excerpt: I got a request for this pudding. Rista Greyvenstein asked me for the pudding. It took me way back. Made me think of the days at Huis Elsabe Joubert in Paarl. We used to get this dessert regularly. The house matron got to serve first and then the rest of us …

seo.title: Post Toasties pudding - Ah, those days in the boarding house - Marinda Kook

html:
I got a request for this pudding. Rista Greyvenstein asked me for the pudding. It took me way back. Made me think of the days at Huis Elsabe Joubert in Paarl. We used to get this dessert regularly. The house matron got to serve first and then the rest of us. You could only hope that by the time the dish reached you there was still a little bit left.

  • 500ml cream
  • 750 ml milk
  • 150 ml custard powder
  • 250 ml sugar
  • 125 ml butter
  • 500ml Post Toasties
  • 5 eggs
  • 125 ml apricot jam
  • 125 ml castor sugar

NOW:

  1. heat the cream, milk and sugar in a pot on the stove. The sugar must dissolve. It mustn't boil.
  2. with a wire whisk, whisk in the custard powder until smooth – no lumps.
  3. separate your eggs and whisk in the egg yolks. Work quickly otherwise you'll end up with scrambled egg.
  4. in another bowl beat the sugar and butter until light and pale.
  5. stir the Post Toasties into the egg and butter mixture.
  6. in a greased ovenproof dish, spoon in a layer of the Post Toasties mixture and then a layer of the custard mixture. Alternate it, but end with a layer of custard mixture.
  7. spread the apricot jam lightly over the top custard layer.
  8. whisk the egg whites and castor sugar together and pipe or spread it right over the top of your pudding.

Bake for around 15 to 20 minutes at 180 degrees Celsius or until your egg-white layer is a lovely golden brown.

A treat that takes you right back!
